choice: Primary metric = probability of success, defined as the fraction of the 5,000 paths in which the household's investable portfolio is never depleted (balance stays > 0) through the last survivor's end-age. Alongside it, every run returns: (a) percentile net-worth bands over time (p10/p25/p50/p75/p90) at each monthly step, (b) a depletion-age distribution (histogram of when/if paths run dry), and (c) the terminal (bequest) net-worth distribution at end-age. These four outputs are the engine's contract to the rest of the app.
rationale: Every downstream area — Scenarios/Active Plan comparison, Net-Worth-Actuals-vs-Plan tracking, and the forecast UX — consumes the engine's output shape, so it must be pinned before those areas are groomed; it's the highest-blast-radius open question left in this area now that the generative process is decided. 'Never depleted' (a hard ruin probability) is the convention ProjectionLab/BoldIn/Root all lead with and matches the spreadsheet's drawdown framing. Percentile bands + depletion-age + terminal distribution cover the three questions a household actually asks (Will I run out? When? What's left?) without committing to richer analytics (e.g., shortfall magnitude, spending-cut feedback) that can layer on later.
